title: Red Sox place Marcelo Mayer on 10-day IL with forearm issue
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox's Marcelo Mayer placed on 10-day IL due to a forearm issue, similar to Isiah Kiner-Falefa.
Tsung-Che Cheng will debut at shortstop as a replacement for Marcelo Mayer against the Yankees.
Red Sox's lineup struggles with injuries including Garrett Crochet, Roman Anthony, and Trevor Story.

title: Bruins reportedly trade for Utah forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins are trading two first-round picks to acquire Utah Mammoth forward JJ Peterka.
Peterka, 24, has a $7.7 million cap hit through 2029-30 and has scored 20-plus goals in three seasons.
The trade includes Bruins’ 2026 first-rounder and a 2028 top 10 protected pick from Florida.

title: Craig Breslow on his job security, whether the Red Sox will buy or sell, and more
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Craig Breslow insists the Boston Red Sox can still turn their season around despite currently being last in the AL East.
Though facing possible trades, Red Sox remain undecided on buying or selling at the August 3 trade deadline.
Breslow is not concerned about his job security, focusing on team improvement and modernizing the front office.

title: Review: ‘Supergirl’ is a hero in search of a better movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Milly Alcock stars as Supergirl, but the film is criticized for being bland and derivative.
The movie features Kara Zor-El and a young girl, Ruthye, on a quest for revenge against Brigands.
Director Craig Gillespie and screenwriter Ana Nogueira include flashbacks to Kara's past for character depth.

title: One of Cambridge’s most beloved Japanese restaurants is closing after 34 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Tampopo, a Japanese restaurant in Cambridge's Porter Exchange, is closing after 34 years as owner Yasumasa Ito retires.
The restaurant, located at 1815 Massachusetts Ave., will serve its last meal on June 30, 2026.
Former employee Kotaro Morita returned from Japan to assist Ito, and customers share memories online.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Wide receivers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown joins the New England Patriots, replacing Stefon Diggs, to enhance their already top-ranked passing attack.
Drake Maye, with Brown as his new primary target, led the league in completion percentage (72) and intended air yards per attempt.
Brown aims for a successful season despite a down year and knee concerns, as he acclimates to the team's playbook.

title: Here’s the Celtics’ reported asking price for Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics are reportedly asking for at least four first-round picks in trade talks for Jaylen Brown.
Boston remains involved in trade discussions after losing out on acquiring Giannis Antetokounmpo from the Bucks.
Jaylen Brown, 29, is under contract for three more years and had one of his best career seasons recently.

title: 4 takeaways from France’s 4-1 win over Norway at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird and Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 at Gillette Stadium, securing first place in Group I of the World Cup.
Ousmane Dembélé scored a first-half hat-trick for France; Norway's Erling Haaland was rested.
Norwegian winger Thelo Aasgaard scored directly after Dembélé's second goal, as Norway showed resilience despite the loss.

title: When Pickles the dog saved the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: In 1966, the Jules Rimet Trophy was stolen in London before being found by a dog named Pickles.
Pickles the dog discovered the World Cup trophy wrapped in newspaper under a car in South London.
Owner David Corbett received a £6,000 reward, and Pickles became a national hero in England.

title: More than 4,000 Mass General Brigham nurses to strike on July 8
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: About 4,500 nurses and clinicians at Mass General Brigham units plan to strike on July 8.
Nurses are demanding competitive wages, affordable health insurance, and better patient care protections.
Mass General Brigham Home Care clinicians will conduct a seven-day strike starting July 8.
